prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: The Neighbor Who Babysat Too Much
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Mother Was Right All Along
Tagline: Love lives here. So does arson.
Theme: Trust isn't given. It's rented.
Omen: a dog barking at an empty gazebo
Relationship dynamic: secret half sibling
Props: blood-adjacent tea towel, boat key, unopened letter

Cast:
- Brooke Hawthorne | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=architect | threat_aura=0.45 | space=old family office described as retreat supplies | secrets=has an unsent email that would destroy a family; buried one traumatic detail under work for years
- Derek Lark | archetype=divorced_redemption_dad | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=marina manager | threat_aura=0.37 | space=marina tackle room described as paperwork I have not gone through yet | secrets=covered for someone out of shame; still has access to a property he does not own
- Tess Pruitt | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=nurse with local intel | threat_aura=0.54 | space=toolshed described as retreat supplies | secrets=knows about a half sibling no one asked for
- Elias Bell | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=therapist | threat_aura=0.65 | space=meditation annex nobody uses in winter | secrets=knows why the old orchard chapel was closed
- Evelyn Hale | archetype=decorative_husband | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=financial advisor | threat_aura=0.63 | space=retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence | secrets=was in the wrong county on the wrong night
- Elias Bennett | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=innkeeper | threat_aura=0.32 | space=meditation annex nobody uses in winter | secrets=saw the wrong person near the lake once and never shut up about it
- Luke Winterson | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Derek Mercer | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Derek Lark is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Brooke Hawthorne says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Alder Ridge.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Derek?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Luke. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Brooke. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The blood-adjacent tea towel is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Elias."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
